originalTitle: Australia news live: vulnerable women and children warned to turn off concealed phones during national emergency alert test next week
content: Some depressing news for your morning. The likelihood of a Reserve Bank interest rate hike has doubled according to market forecasts, as the escalating Middle East crisis once again drives fuel prices higher amid dwindling global oil reserves. The total breakdown in the fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran has sent the international Brent crude benchmark surging by 23% over the past two weeks and back within reach of $US90 a barrel. Read the story here: International student charged over alleged $5m luxury watch fraud A 28-year-old international student has been charged with allegedly exploiting the Tourist Refund Scheme (TRS) at Australian airports by lodging more than $5m in fraudulent claims on luxury and counterfeit watches. In Australia, international travellers can claim a GST refund for purchased goods worth more than $300, provided they’re not brought back into Australia. The Australian Federal Police and Australian Border Force allege the man travelled more than 20 times to Chengdu Tianfu Airport in China between February last year and May, often remaining overseas for less than 24 hours, and deposited more than $1.1m in cash into Australian bank accounts. They allege the man edited bank documents and made repeated claims for items not eligible for the TRS. Warrants were executed in May and about $270,000 in cash was seized. He was charged with dishonestly intending to obtain a gain, facing a maximum penalty of 10 years in prison if convicted. He is due to appear before Downing Centre Local Court today. Controversial new child protection laws passed by the Northern Territory government have been greeted with dismay by First Nations advocates and concern from other national and state leaders, Australian Associated Press reports. The NT legislative assembly passed the Every Child Matters bill late Tuesday night, in what it said was the most significant overhaul of its child protection system in a generation. The new law abolishes the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children placement principle, which accords that First Nations people who are removed from their homes are kept within their own culture. The government claimed that the reforms tackled the root causes of crime, protected children, strengthened families, and would break the cycle that saw young people fall through the cracks of the child protection system. The children placement principle emerged in recognition of the profound harms caused to the Stolen Generations and has been recognised in child welfare legislation since the early 1980s. The new laws were passed on the same day as an interim report was handed down into the territory’s child protection laws in response to the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby, who was abducted from a town camp in Alice Springs in April. The legislation from the ruling Country Liberal party has been condemned by the federal government, state leaders and First Nations leaders. The federal Indigenous Australians minister, Malarndirri McCarthy, said the “Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Child Placement Principle is based on evidence and partnership and should not be disregarded”. Catherine Liddle, CEO of the Secretariat of National Aboriginal and Islander Child Care, said the laws would cause further harm to First Nations children. AI an ‘existential challenge’ for journalism, warns Reuters editor-in-chief in Andrew Olle media lecture Journalism faces an existential challenge from AI as news consumers bypass journalists in favour of chatbots to find information, the editor-in-chief of Reuters, Alessandra Galloni, has warned in her Andrew Olle media lecture. She said: After two decades of battling the advertising drain of social media platforms and algorithms, the scourge of misinformation, and the slow creep of news avoidance, we now face a new, potentially existential challenge. Galloni’s comments came on the same day Australia’s biggest media company, Nine Entertainment, blamed the “extreme state of disruption” from AI for its decision to cut another 30 newsroom jobs at the Sydney Morning Herald and the Age Galloni, who oversees Reuters’ global editorial operations, said AI is reshaping how we seek, consume and monetise information” Today, 10% of news consumers globally use AI chatbots to get their news. That is nearly 50% more than a year ago, according to the Reuters Institute Digital News Report 2026. Among people under 35, that figure is 16% – and these numbers are almost certainly undercounts, as they do not include AI-generated summaries in Google, embedded AI in news apps, or the AI your phone surfaces before you even open a browser. Named in honour of the late ABC broadcaster Andrew Olle, the lecture is an annual forum for examining the role and future of journalism. The 2026 Andrew Olle Media Lecture is available on ABC iview. Karen Bentley, the CEO of Wesnet, the national peak body for specialist women’s domestic and family violence services, is providing advice for women and children experiencing domestic and family violence, who may have hidden phones as part of their safety plan. The national test will be a critical alert, meaning it will sound through a device that is on silent or do not disturb. She says: Wesnet’s recommendation is that on Monday, if you do have a concealed or hidden phone, to support your safety, that you consider turning that device off or switching it into airplane mode, one hour before the national test on Monday, and to keep it off or in airplane mode for 24 hours … Once the national emergency alert system is officially in place from October, people using hidden phones, as part of a safety plan, will need to make their own decision about the risks of having their phones on, on a permanent basis or whether it’s safer for them to keep their hidden phones off when they’re not in use. chineseTitle: 澳大利亚新闻直播：下周国家紧急警报测试期间，弱势妇女和儿童被警告关闭隐藏的电话
chineseContent: 早上有一些令人沮丧的消息。根据市场预测，随着中东危机不断升级，在全球石油储备不断减少的情况下，燃油价格再次上涨，储备银行加息的可能性增加了一倍。美国和伊朗之间脆弱的停火协议彻底破裂，导致国际布伦特原油基准价格在过去两周飙升 23%，回到每桶 90 美元的水平。阅读此处的报道：国际学生因涉嫌价值 500 万美元的豪华手表欺诈而被指控 一名 28 岁的国际学生被指控涉嫌利用澳大利亚机场的游客退款计划 (TRS)，针对豪华和假冒手表提出超过 500 万美元的欺诈索赔。在澳大利亚，国际旅客可以对购买的价值超过 300 美元的商品申请商品及服务税退税，前提是这些商品没有带回澳大利亚。澳大利亚联邦警察和澳大利亚边防局称，该男子去年2月至5月期间前往中国成都天府机场20多次，通常在海外停留不到24小时，并向澳大利亚银行账户存入超过110万澳元现金。他们指控该男子编辑了银行文件，并多次对不符合 TRS 资格的物品提出索赔。逮捕令于 5 月执行，并扣押了约 27 万美元现金。他被指控不诚实地意图获取利益，如果罪名成立，最高将面临10年监禁。他将于今天在唐宁中心地方法院出庭。据澳大利亚美联社报道，北领地政府通过的有争议的新儿童保护法引起了原住民倡导者的沮丧和其他国家和州领导人的关注。北领地立法
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
